label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the options is true based on the passage. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand the details. Starting with option A: "In Thailand people give fish and birds to the birthday person as presents." The passage says in Thailand there's a custom where they buy live fish and birds for the birthday person, and then the person frees the animals, which brings good luck. So that sounds like they do give fish and birds as presents, so A might be correct. But let me check the other options. Option B: "In Holland people give presents to unbirthday persons on their birthdays." The passage states in Holland, children not only get presents but also give things out to classmates and teachers on their birthdays. So the ones celebrating their birthday give out presents, not to "unbirthday" persons, which I think is not mentioned here. So B is probably incorrect. Option C: "In Nigeria people who were born in the same period may celebrate their birthdays on the same day." The passage says in Nigeria, people in a certain age group celebrate their birthdays together on a certain day. So that matches option C, which says same period (age group) may celebrate on same day. So
